the sulfur cucumber; the most common sea cucumber taken during the expedition, and the most widespread and abundant sea cucumber in the Gulf today. Ricketts’s field notes from this station read, “present by the millions.” One specimen had a commensal pearl fish living in it, Encheliophiops hancocki Reid.
